home.problemTitle: Most AI projects fail before they start.
home.problemP1: The problem isn't access to AI tools. ChatGPT is free. Copilot is built into your Office subscription. The problem is that nobody has redesigned the workflows those tools are supposed to improve.
home.problemP2: Consultants deliver a roadmap and leave. Vendors deliver software and leave. Your team gets a login and a manual, and within three months the tool is abandoned.
home.problemP3: Fetch stays until the AI is embedded in how your team actually works — and until you can measure the difference.

careers.heroTitle: Build the future of work in Vietnam.
careers.heroDesc: We're a small, focused team doing serious work. If you want to be at the centre of Vietnam's AI transformation — not watching from the sidelines — this is where you belong.
careers.perk1Desc: Every project changes how a real business operates. You'll see the results of your work within weeks, not quarters.
careers.perk2Desc: We're growing quickly. The people who join now will lead the next phase. There's no ceiling here.

careers.speculativeDesc: We're always interested in exceptional people. Send us a note about what you do and why you'd be a good fit.
careers.noRoleDesc: We hire on attitude and ability. If you're sharp, driven, and want to work on AI transformation in Vietnam, send your CV and tell us what you can contribute.
careers.job1Req1: Bachelor's degree in Game Design, Computer Science, Business Administration or related field; Master's degree is a strong advantage.
careers.job1Req2: 1–2 years of experience as a business analyst in the software industry, focused on game development projects.
careers.job1Req3: Strong understanding of game design principles, including 2D and 3D design concepts, animation, and visual effects.
careers.job1Req4: Experience with game development tools and platforms such as Cocos, Spine, etc.
careers.job1Req5: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, able to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
careers.job1Req6: Passion for gaming and creative thinking with the ability to think outside the box.
careers.job2Req1: Bachelor's degree in Project Management, Information Systems, Business Administration or related field.
careers.job2Req2: 4+ years of project management experience, preferably in gaming or related creative industries.
careers.job2Req3: Strong understanding of project management methodologies, tools and techniques.
careers.job2Req4: Ability to identify and resolve problems effectively.
careers.job2Req5: Excellent communication, translation and relationship skills to connect multicultural teams.
careers.job2Req6: High sense of responsibility, adaptable, and able to multitask in a dynamic environment.
careers.job3Req1: 7+ years of professional experience in full-stack development.
careers.job3Req2: Proficient in React.js, React Native and Node.js with 4 years of full-time experience or equivalent.
careers.job3Req3: Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases (PostgreSQL, MongoDB).
careers.job3Req4: Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and microservices architecture.
careers.job3Req5: Experience with cloud services (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
careers.job4Req1: 1–3 years of experience in an administrative or office role.
careers.job4Req2: Excellent organisational and time management skills.
careers.job4Req3: Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
careers.job4Req4: Good communication skills in Vietnamese and English.
careers.job4Req5: Proactive attitude and ability to work independently.
careers.job5Req1: Bachelor's degree in Project Management, Information Systems or related field.
careers.job5Req2: 3+ years of experience managing technology or digital transformation projects.
careers.job5Req4: Excellent communication skills with clients and internal teams.
careers.job5Req6: Experience working with SMEs is an advantage.
careers.job6Req1: 2+ years of UI/UX design experience for web or mobile applications.
careers.job6Req2: Proficient in Figma and modern design tools.
careers.job6Req3: Understanding of responsive design and accessibility principles.
careers.job6Req5: Ability to work closely with the development team.
careers.job7Req1: 5+ years building production-grade applications with Node.js and modern frontend frameworks (Vue.js preferred) in TypeScript.
careers.job7Req2: Deep expertise in designing, deploying and maintaining microservices architectures.
careers.job7Req3: Solid hands-on experience with Kafka, gRPC, and event-driven systems.
careers.job7Req4: Proficient in MySQL, Elasticsearch, and Redis.
careers.job7Req5: Familiar with MS Azure and infrastructure orchestration tools like Kubernetes and Helm.
careers.job7Req6: Deep understanding of data structures, distributed systems, and system design principles.
careers.job7Req7: Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience in technical leadership and mentoring.
careers.job7Req8: Strong adherence to Agile methodology and software development best practices.
careers.job8Req3: Proficient in Vietnamese and English (reading, writing, speaking).
careers.job8Req4: Strong financial acumen, business analysis skills, and proficiency in report writing.

about.heroTitle: We exist to make AI transformation actually work.
about.heroDesc: Not the strategy. Not the roadmap. The actual change in how your team works every day.
about.storyTitle: Built from frustration with how AI projects fail.
about.storyP1: Fetch was founded after watching too many AI projects fail the same way: a consultant delivers a strategy deck, a vendor installs software, and six months later nothing has changed. The tools exist. The will exists. The execution doesn't.
about.storyP2: We built Fetch to be the execution layer. We embed into your business, redesign the workflows, deploy the tools, train the team, and stay until the AI is actually being used. Then we measure it.
about.storyP3: We operate in Vietnam with a local team that understands the market, the regulatory environment, and the specific challenges Vietnamese enterprises face when adopting AI.
about.value1Desc: Anyone can write a roadmap. We stay until the AI is embedded in your operations.
about.value2Desc: Every engagement ends with a documented ROI summary. If we can't measure it, we haven't done our job.
about.value3Desc: If AI isn't the right solution for your problem, we'll tell you. We'd rather lose the engagement than waste your time.
about.locationTitle: Based in Vietnam. Focused on Vietnam.

pods.heroTitle: The execution model that makes AI adoption work
pods.heroDesc: A transformation pod is a compact, multi-role execution team that embeds directly into your business to deliver AI adoption — not advise on it.
pods.whatIsPodTitle: Not consultants. Not contractors. An execution team.
pods.whatIsPodDesc1: A Fetch transformation pod is a small, focused team assembled specifically for your engagement. Each pod includes the roles required to deliver your transformation — strategy, analysis, implementation, and change management.
pods.whatIsPodDesc2: Pods work inside your operations, not from a distance. They interview your team, map your workflows, deploy tools in your environment, and train your people. When the engagement ends, the results stay.

caseStudies.heroDesc: These are anonymised accounts of actual Fetch engagements. The numbers are real. The situations are real. Names and identifying details have been changed.
caseStudies.patternTitle: The same three things show up in every engagement.
caseStudies.ctaTitle: Want to see what this looks like for your business?
caseStudies.ctaDesc: Book a 45-minute strategy call. We’ll assess your workflows and tell you honestly what a Fetch engagement would look like.

patterns.title: The problem was never the AI tool
patterns.body: In every case, the client already had access to AI tools. ChatGPT was free. Copilot was built into their Office subscription. The problem was that nobody had redesigned the workflows those tools were supposed to improve. We always start with workflow mapping, not tool deployment.

patterns.title: The first failure point is always adoption
patterns.body: Every client who had previously attempted an AI implementation had failed at adoption. The tool was deployed, the team was given a login, and within weeks they’d reverted to their manual processes. We treat adoption as the primary deliverable, not an afterthought.

patterns.title: The ROI is measurable within 60 days
patterns.body: In every engagement, we establish a baseline measurement before we start and track time savings weekly post-deployment. The ROI is never theoretical — it’s measured in hours recovered and costs eliminated.

studies.headline: A commercial fleet operator cut route planning time by 70% and reduced fuel costs by $190K annually.
studies.subheadline: 30-Day AI Adoption Sprint · Ho Chi Minh City · 120 vehicles, 18 operations staff
studies.situation:
The operations team at a Ho Chi Minh City-based commercial vehicle fleet company was managing 120 vehicles across multiple client contracts. Route planning was done manually each morning by three senior dispatchers, each spending two to three hours building daily schedules in spreadsheets and cross-referencing against driver availability, vehicle maintenance windows, and client delivery windows.

Beyond planning, the team was drowning in administrative overhead: driver briefing documents, client delivery reports, fuel reconciliation, and compliance paperwork for regulatory requirements. The operations manager estimated that 60% of her team’s day was spent on tasks that required no judgement — just data entry, formatting, and distribution.

The company had explored off-the-shelf fleet management software but found the implementation costs prohibitive and the tools too rigid for their mix of contract types. They needed AI that worked within their existing systems, not a platform replacement.
whatWeDid: Spent the first week embedded with the dispatch team, riding along on the morning planning process and documenting every step. Identified that route planning alone was consuming 63 dispatcher-hours per week — the single largest time sink in the operation.

Redesigned the route planning workflow to use AI-assisted optimisation within the team’s existing tools, eliminating the manual spreadsheet process while preserving dispatcher control over final decisions.
Automated the generation of driver briefing documents, client delivery reports, and fuel reconciliation summaries — three high-volume, low-judgement tasks consuming an estimated 20 hours per week.
Ran two structured training sessions with all 18 operations staff, including a dispatcher-specific playbook. Tracked adoption and time savings daily for the first three weeks post-deployment.

results.label: reduction in daily route planning time
annual fuel cost reduction from optimised routes
to full deployment across all contracts

studies.quote: Our dispatchers were spending half their morning building routes that AI can now draft in minutes. They still make the final call — but they’re making it in 20 minutes instead of two hours.

studies.headline: A freight forwarder recovered 14 hours per operations manager per week.
studies.subheadline: 30-Day AI Adoption Sprint · Ho Chi Minh City · 45 staff
studies.situation:
The operations team at a mid-sized Vietnamese freight forwarder was spending the majority of their working day on tasks that required no judgement: pulling shipment status updates from carrier portals, copying data into customer emails, chasing vendors for ETAs, and compiling weekly performance reports from five different spreadsheets.

Each operations manager was spending an estimated 3.2 hours per day on these tasks. With seven managers, that was 22 hours of senior operational capacity lost every day to copy-paste work.

The company had purchased a ChatGPT Enterprise licence six months earlier. Nobody was using it in a structured way.
whatWeDid: Spent week one embedded with the operations team, documenting every workflow step-by-step. We found 23 distinct manual touchpoints across the shipment lifecycle.
Scored each touchpoint against our AI opportunity framework: impact, feasibility, and implementation speed. Identified four high-priority workflows for immediate redesign.
Redesigned and deployed two workflows in weeks three and four: automated shipment status aggregation and customer email generation; AI-assisted vendor communication and ETA tracking.
Ran three training sessions with the operations team, including a role-specific playbook for each manager. Tracked adoption daily for the first two weeks post-deployment.
studies.quote: We’d been talking about AI for 18 months. Fetch came in, mapped our operations in a week, and had two workflows running in production by week four. The team actually uses them.

studies.headline: A boutique consulting firm eliminated $180K in annual admin cost.
studies.subheadline: AI Implementation + Team Upskilling · Hà Nội · 28 consultants
studies.situation:
The firm’s 28 consultants were collectively spending an estimated 40% of their billable hours on non-billable administrative work: writing proposals from scratch for similar engagements, reconciling billing across multiple clients, compiling client reports from raw data, and drafting status updates.

The managing director had calculated that if she could recover half of that time and redirect it to billable work, the firm would generate an additional $360K in annual revenue without hiring a single additional person.

The challenge was that every consultant worked slightly differently. There was no standardised process for proposals, no template library, and no shared knowledge base. Any AI solution had to work across the existing diversity of working styles.
whatWeDid: Conducted a workflow audit across all 28 consultants over two weeks, identifying the five highest-volume administrative tasks and documenting the variation in how each was performed.
Built a shared prompt library and template system covering proposal generation, billing reconciliation, client reporting, and status updates.
Integrated the AI tooling with the firm’s existing project management and billing systems, eliminating the need for manual data transfer.

Ran four structured upskilling sessions over two weeks, with role-specific playbooks. Followed up with a 30-day adoption check-in and additional coaching for the three consultants who were slowest to adopt.
studies.quote: Our consultants were spending 40% of their time on admin. After the sprint, that’s down to about 15%. The ROI calculation was straightforward — we saw payback in the first month.

studies.headline: A regional financial services firm cut compliance documentation time by 58%.
studies.subheadline: Workflow Redesign + AI Implementation · Ho Chi Minh City · 3 business units
studies.situation:
The firm’s compliance and operations teams were producing a high volume of documentation: client onboarding packs, KYC summaries, regulatory filing narratives, and internal audit reports. Each document required significant manual effort.

The head of operations estimated that compliance documentation was consuming 35% of the operations team’s capacity. During peak regulatory periods, the team was regularly working evenings and weekends to meet deadlines.

The firm had previously attempted to implement an AI tool. The project stalled after three months because the tool had been deployed without redesigning the underlying workflows, and the team had reverted to their manual processes.
whatWeDid: Started with a workflow redesign engagement before any AI was deployed. Documented the existing documentation processes in detail, identified the bottlenecks, and redesigned the workflows to be AI-ready.
Identified that the previous AI implementation had failed because it had been layered on top of broken processes. The new design eliminated three redundant steps before automation was introduced.
Deployed AI-assisted document generation across three workflows: client onboarding packs, KYC summaries, and regulatory filing narratives.

Worked closely with the compliance team to ensure all AI-generated outputs met regulatory standards. Built a review and sign-off process that maintained human oversight while eliminating the manual drafting work.
studies.quote: What I appreciated was that they pushed back when we wanted to automate the wrong things. They redesigned the process first. That’s the reason it worked.

articles.title: How Vietnamese SMEs should adopt AI without breaking operations
articles.excerpt: Most AI adoption advice is written for large enterprises with dedicated transformation teams. This guide is for Vietnamese SMEs that need to adopt AI while keeping the business running.
articles.body:
Most AI adoption advice assumes you have a dedicated transformation team, a six-figure implementation budget, and the luxury of running a pilot that doesn't affect day-to-day operations. Vietnamese SMEs rarely have any of those things.

**The core challenge for SMEs**

The challenge isn't access to AI tools — ChatGPT is free, Copilot is built into Office, and there are hundreds of AI-powered SaaS products targeting every business function. The challenge is that adopting AI while running a business requires you to change how work gets done without stopping the work.

**Start with one workflow, not a strategy**

The most common mistake SMEs make is trying to develop an "AI strategy" before they've deployed anything. Strategy is useful once you have evidence. Before that, it's speculation. Pick one workflow that is repetitive, time-consuming, and well-documented. Map how it currently works. Then redesign it with AI in the loop.

**The three-week rule**

If an AI tool isn't embedded in your team's daily workflow within three weeks of deployment, it won't be. The window for adoption is short. This means the tool needs to be configured for your specific context, your team needs structured training (not just a login), and someone needs to be accountable for adoption metrics.

**Measure from day one**

Before you deploy anything, agree on what you're measuring. Time saved per task. Error rate reduction. Volume handled without additional headcount. These numbers are what justify the next investment and what tell you whether the first one worked.

articles.title: What an AI transformation pod actually does
articles.excerpt: The pod model is different from consulting and different from staffing. This article explains exactly how a transformation pod works inside a business and why the model produces better outcomes.
articles.body:
The transformation pod model sits between two things that don't work: strategy consulting (which produces recommendations but doesn't implement them) and software vendors (which implement tools but don't change how work gets done).

**What a pod is**

A transformation pod is a small, embedded team — typically two to four people — that works inside your business for a defined period. They're not there to advise. They're there to do: map workflows, configure tools, train teams, and measure outcomes.

**The four-week cycle**

Week one is workflow mapping. Not how you think your operations work — how they actually work. The gap between the two is almost always significant, and it's the reason most AI implementations fail.

Week two and three are configuration and deployment. The pod selects and configures tools against the mapped workflow, integrates them with existing systems, and runs structured training sessions with the team that will use them.

Week four is measurement and handover. The pod tracks adoption rates and time savings, produces an ROI summary, and hands over a roadmap for what to do next.

**Why it works**

The pod model works because it combines the things that consulting and software vendors each do well, while eliminating the handover problem. There's no point at which the pod says "here's your strategy" or "here's your software" and leaves.

articles.title: AI workflow redesign vs buying more software
articles.excerpt: Buying AI software without redesigning workflows is one of the most common and expensive mistakes in enterprise AI adoption. Here's why workflow redesign must come first.
articles.body:
The most common pattern in failed AI implementations is this: a business buys an AI tool, deploys it on top of existing workflows, and then wonders why the team isn't using it.

**The workflow problem**

AI tools are designed to automate or assist with specific tasks. If the workflow those tasks sit within is poorly designed — with redundant steps, unclear ownership, and manual handoffs — the AI tool will automate the wrong things or fail to integrate with how work actually gets done.

**Redesign before you automate**

The right sequence is: document the current workflow, identify the bottlenecks and inefficiencies, redesign the workflow to be AI-ready, and then select and deploy tools against the redesigned workflow. This sequence is slower upfront but produces dramatically better outcomes.

**The cost of skipping redesign**

Businesses that skip workflow redesign and go straight to tool deployment typically spend 3-4x more on implementation, achieve 40-60% lower adoption rates, and abandon the tool within six months. The cost of redesign is almost always lower than the cost of a failed implementation.

articles.title: Why AI upskilling fails and how to do it properly
articles.excerpt: Most AI training programmes fail because they focus on tools rather than workflows. This article explains what effective AI upskilling looks like and how to measure it.
articles.body:
The standard approach to AI upskilling is a one-day workshop, a login to a new tool, and a hope that the team figures it out. This approach has a predictable outcome: the tool gets used for a week and then abandoned.

**Why standard training fails**

Standard AI training fails for three reasons. First, it focuses on tool features rather than workflow application — teams learn what the tool can do in the abstract, not how to use it in their specific daily work. Second, it doesn't account for the change management dimension — adoption is as much about behaviour change as it is about technical knowledge. Third, it doesn't build in accountability — there's no measurement of whether the training translated into changed behaviour.

**What effective upskilling looks like**

Effective AI upskilling has four components: workflow-specific training (not generic tool training), structured practice in the actual work context, a designated AI champion who supports adoption after training, and measurement of adoption metrics at 30 and 60 days.

**The champion model**

The most effective upskilling programmes identify and develop AI champions within each team — people who become the go-to resource for their colleagues and who are accountable for adoption outcomes. Champions need both technical depth and change management skills.

articles.title: Automating operations without losing control
articles.excerpt: Operations leaders worry that AI automation will create black boxes they can't manage. Here's how to design AI-assisted operations that maintain visibility and control.
articles.body:
The most common concern operations leaders have about AI automation is loss of control. If AI is making decisions or taking actions in your operations, how do you know what's happening and why?

**Design for transparency**

The answer is to design AI-assisted workflows with transparency as a first principle. Every AI action should be logged. Every AI decision should be explainable. Every AI output should be reviewable by a human before it triggers downstream action.

**The human-in-the-loop principle**

For high-stakes operational decisions — route changes, exception handling, vendor escalations — AI should assist rather than decide. The AI drafts the recommendation; the human approves it. This maintains accountability while still capturing the efficiency gains.

**Monitoring and intervention**

Effective AI-assisted operations require monitoring dashboards that surface anomalies and exceptions for human review. The goal is not to remove humans from the loop but to focus human attention where it adds the most value — on exceptions, edge cases, and decisions that require judgement.

articles.title: Why enterprise AI implementations fail
articles.excerpt: Most enterprise AI implementations fail to deliver the expected returns. This article examines the five most common failure modes and how to avoid them.
articles.body:
Enterprise AI implementation has a high failure rate. Estimates vary, but most research suggests that 70-80% of enterprise AI projects fail to deliver the expected business value. Understanding why they fail is the first step to avoiding the same mistakes.

**Failure mode 1: Automating broken processes**

The most common failure mode is deploying AI on top of poorly designed processes. AI amplifies what's already there — if the underlying process is inefficient or poorly structured, AI will make it efficiently inefficient.

**Failure mode 2: Underestimating the change management requirement**

AI implementation is a change management challenge as much as a technical one. Teams that have been doing work a certain way for years don't change their behaviour because a new tool is available. Adoption requires structured training, clear accountability, and sustained support.

**Failure mode 3: Measuring the wrong things**

Many AI implementations are declared successful based on technical metrics (the tool is deployed, the integration works) rather than business metrics (time saved, cost reduced, quality improved). If you're not measuring business outcomes from day one, you won't know whether the implementation worked.

**Failure mode 4: Insufficient stakeholder alignment**

AI implementations that lack clear executive sponsorship and cross-functional alignment consistently underperform. Without visible leadership commitment and clear ownership, adoption stalls and the implementation loses momentum.

**Failure mode 5: Scope creep**

Starting with too broad a scope is a reliable path to failure. The most successful implementations start with one workflow, prove the model, and then scale. Trying to transform multiple workflows simultaneously almost always results in none of them being done well.

articles.title: What AI-ready leadership looks like
articles.excerpt: AI transformation requires a specific kind of leadership. This article defines what AI-ready leadership looks like and how executives can develop it.
articles.body:
AI transformation is a leadership challenge before it's a technology challenge. The organisations that successfully adopt AI are almost always led by executives who have a clear and specific understanding of what AI can and can't do, and who are willing to make the structural changes required to capture the value.

**What AI-ready leaders understand**

AI-ready leaders understand that AI is not a technology project — it's an operational change programme. They understand that the bottleneck is almost never the technology; it's the workflow design, the change management, and the adoption. They understand that ROI from AI is measurable and that they should be measuring it.

**What AI-ready leaders do differently**

AI-ready leaders start with specific operational problems rather than general AI ambitions. They invest in workflow documentation before they invest in AI tools. They treat adoption metrics as a leadership accountability, not an IT metric. They identify and develop AI champions within their teams.

**How to develop AI readiness**

The most effective path to AI readiness for executives is direct exposure to a working AI implementation — not a demo, but a real deployment in a real operational context. Understanding what it actually takes to get AI working in an organisation is the foundation for leading the transformation.

tools.title: AI Readiness Assessment
tools.desc: A structured self-assessment covering workflow documentation, data quality, change management capacity, and leadership alignment. Identify your gaps before you start.

tools.title: Workflow Mapping Template
tools.desc: A step-by-step template for documenting operational workflows in the format required for AI redesign. Includes worked examples from logistics, professional services, and financial services.

tools.title: AI Adoption Sprint Playbook
tools.desc: The complete 30-day playbook used by Fetch transformation pods. Covers workflow mapping, tool configuration, team training, and ROI measurement.

tools.title: Team Upskilling Framework
tools.desc: A four-session training framework for upskilling teams during AI implementation. Includes session outlines, exercises, and adoption measurement tools.

tools.title: AI ROI Calculator
tools.desc: A spreadsheet model for calculating the ROI of operational AI implementations. Input your baseline metrics and implementation costs to project payback period and three-year ROI.

tools.title: AI Governance Policy Template
tools.desc: A customisable policy template covering AI use, data governance, and accountability. Designed for Vietnamese enterprises and aligned with local regulatory requirements.

grants.heroTitle: The government is paying for your AI transformation.
grants.heroDesc: Vietnam has committed VND 30 trillion to AI adoption. Three active programs cover up to 70% of qualifying project costs. Fetch helps you access them.
grants.ctaTitle: Find out which grants you qualify for.
grants.ctaDesc: Book a 45-minute strategy call. We'll review your business, identify the right programs, and tell you exactly what to prepare.

grants.name: National AI Development Fund
grants.nameVi: Quỹ Phát triển AI Quốc gia
eligibility: Annual revenue under VND 300 billion (SME threshold)
Project involves AI deployment, automation, or digital workflow redesign
process: Prepare project proposal with ROI projections
grants.note: Fetch assists with the full application process as part of our engagement. Most clients qualify within the first strategy call.

grants.name: SME Digital Transformation Program
grants.nameVi: Chương trình Chuyển đổi số cho DNNVV
eligibility: Fewer than 300 employees OR revenue under VND 300 billion
Has not previously received equivalent digital transformation support
Willing to participate in outcome reporting
covered: Free digital readiness assessment (valued VND 50-100 million)
Access to national digital transformation platform
process: Register on the national SME portal
grants.note: Fetch is an approved consulting partner under this programme. We can register you directly and begin the assessment as part of our onboarding.

grants.name: Technology Investment Tax Incentive
grants.nameVi: Ưu đãi thuế đầu tư công nghệ
eligibility: SME investing in technology or AI innovation
Project aligns with national digital transformation priorities
covered: Full CIT exemption for first 2 years of qualifying investment
R&D expense deduction at 150% of actual cost
process: Document technology investment with receipts and contracts
File CIT return with supporting schedules
Incentive applied to current and future tax years
grants.note: We work with your accountant or tax advisor to ensure all AI transformation costs are correctly classified to maximise your tax benefit.

faqs.q: Can a foreign-owned company operating in Vietnam access these grants?
faqs.a: Yes — foreign-invested enterprises (FIEs) registered in Vietnam are eligible for most programs, including the SME Digital Transformation Program and the tax incentives. The National AI Fund has some additional requirements for FIEs; we assess this on a case-by-case basis.

faqs.q: How long does the grant application process take?
faqs.a: The SME Digital Transformation Program can be initiated within 1-2 weeks. The National AI Fund review takes 30-45 days from submission. Tax incentives are applied at year-end filing. We handle the paperwork — you focus on running your business.

faqs.q: Do we need to apply for grants before starting the AI transformation?
faqs.a: For the National AI Fund, yes — approval must precede expenditure. For the SME program and tax incentives, costs can often be backdated to the start of the financial year. We advise on timing during the strategy call.

faqs.q: What if we don't qualify for any grants?
faqs.a: Our 30-Day AI Sprint typically delivers ROI within the first month regardless of grant support. The grants reduce your cost, but the business case stands without them. We'll tell you honestly in the first call.

faqs.q: Does Fetch charge extra for grant application support?
faqs.a: No. Grant application support is included in all our engagements at no additional cost. We've done this enough times that it's part of our standard process.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.effectiveDate: Effective date: 1 April 2025 · Fetch Technology Vietnam
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of the Fetch Technology website and your engagement with Fetch Technology Pte. Ltd. for services. Please read them carefully. If you have questions, contact us at legal@fetch.tech before proceeding.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
sections.content:
By accessing or using the Fetch Technology website (fetch.tech) or engaging Fetch Technology Pte. Ltd. ("Fetch", "we", "us", or "our") for services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, please do not use our website or services.

These Terms apply to all visitors, users, and clients. They should be read alongside our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated by reference.

sections.title: 2. Services
sections.content:
Fetch Technology provides AI transformation and workflow implementation services to businesses, including but not limited to:

— Workflow mapping and AI opportunity assessment
— AI tool selection, configuration, and deployment
— Team upskilling and training programmes
— Ongoing support and optimisation
— The 30-Day AI Adoption Sprint and other packaged engagements

The specific scope, deliverables, timelines, and fees for any engagement are set out in a separate Statement of Work or Service Agreement signed by both parties. These Terms govern the general relationship between Fetch and its clients and website users.

sections.title: 3. Website use
sections.content:
You may use our website for lawful purposes only. You agree not to:

— Use the website in any way that violates applicable laws or regulations
— Attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of the website or its underlying systems
— Transmit any unsolicited or unauthorised advertising or promotional material
— Introduce any malicious code, viruses, or other harmful material
— Scrape, copy, or reproduce website content without our prior written consent
— Use the website to impersonate any person or entity

We reserve the right to restrict or terminate access to the website for any user who violates these Terms.

sections.title: 4. Intellectual property
sections.content:
All content on this website — including text, graphics, logos, images, and software — is the property of Fetch Technology Vietnam or its content suppliers and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws.

You may view and print pages from the website for your personal, non-commercial use. You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, create derivative works from, or commercially exploit any content from this website without our prior written consent.

Any methodologies, frameworks, tools, templates, or processes developed by Fetch in the course of delivering services remain the intellectual property of Fetch, unless otherwise agreed in writing. Clients receive a licence to use deliverables for their internal business purposes.

sections.title: 5. Client engagements
sections.content:
When you engage Fetch for services, the following general terms apply unless superseded by a signed Service Agreement:

— **Payment:** Fees are as set out in the relevant Statement of Work. Invoices are payable within 14 days of issue unless otherwise agreed.
— **Confidentiality:** Both parties agree to keep confidential any proprietary information disclosed during the engagement. This obligation survives termination of the engagement.
— **Client responsibilities:** Clients are responsible for providing timely access to relevant systems, data, and personnel. Delays caused by the client may affect timelines and deliverables.
— **Warranties:** Fetch warrants that services will be performed with reasonable skill and care. We do not warrant specific business outcomes, as these depend on factors outside our control including client team adoption and organisational change.
— **Termination:** Either party may terminate an engagement with 14 days’ written notice. Fees for work completed up to the termination date remain payable.

sections.title: 6. Limitation of liability
sections.content:
To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:

— Fetch’s total liability to you for any claim arising out of or in connection with these Terms or our services shall not exceed the total fees paid by you to Fetch in the three months preceding the claim.
— Fetch shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, loss of data, or loss of business opportunity, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.
— Nothing in these Terms limits or excludes liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, fraud, or any other liability that cannot be excluded by law.

sections.title: 7. Indemnification
sections.content:
You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Fetch Technology Vietnam and its directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or in connection with:

— Your use of our website or services in violation of these Terms
— Your violation of any applicable law or regulation
— Any content or information you provide to us that is inaccurate, misleading, or infringes the rights of any third party

sections.title: 8. Third-party links and tools
sections.content:
Our website may contain links to third-party websites or references to third-party tools and services. These links are provided for your convenience only. We do not endorse, control, or take responsibility for the content, privacy practices, or terms of any third-party website or service.

In the course of delivering services, we may recommend or deploy third-party AI tools and software. The use of such tools is subject to the terms and conditions of the respective third-party providers. Fetch is not responsible for the performance, availability, or terms of third-party tools.

sections.title: 9. Disclaimers
sections.content:
Our website and its content are provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis without any warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

We do not warrant that the website will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of viruses or other harmful components. We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the website at any time without notice.

sections.title: 10. Governing law and dispute resolution
sections.content:
These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Vietnam, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms, including any question regarding their existence, validity, or termination, shall first be referred to mediation in accordance with Vietnamese law. If the dispute is not resolved through mediation within 60 days, it shall be referred to and finally resolved by the competent courts of Vietnam.

sections.title: 11. Changes to these terms
sections.content:
We reserve the right to update these Terms at any time. We will post the revised Terms on this page with an updated effective date. Your continued use of our website or services after any changes constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

For material changes that affect ongoing client engagements, we will provide reasonable notice.

sections.title: 12. Contact
sections.content:
If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us:

**Fetch Technology Vietnam**
Email: legal@fetch.tech
Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.effectiveDate: Effective date: 1 April 2025 · Fetch Technology Vietnam
privacy.intro: Fetch Technology Vietnam is committed to protecting your personal data. This policy describes what data we collect, why we collect it, and how we use and protect it — in plain language, without unnecessary legal jargon.

sections.title: 1. Who we are
sections.content:
Fetch Technology Pte. Ltd. ("Fetch", "we", "us", or "our") is a company incorporated in Singapore. We provide AI transformation and workflow implementation services to businesses. Our registered address is in Singapore.

This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal data when you visit our website (fetch.tech), enquire about our services, or engage us as a client.

sections.title: 2. What personal data we collect
sections.content:
We collect personal data that you provide directly to us and data collected automatically when you use our website.

**Data you provide directly:**
— Name, job title, and company name when you submit an enquiry or book a call
— Email address and phone number when you contact us
— Information you share during discovery calls, strategy sessions, or client engagements
— Any other information you choose to provide in correspondence with us

**Data collected automatically:**
— IP address and approximate location
— Browser type, operating system, and device information
— Pages visited, time spent on pages, and referring URLs
— Anonymised analytics data collected via our website analytics provider

sections.title: 3. How we use your personal data
sections.content:
We use your personal data for the following purposes:

— To respond to your enquiries and schedule calls or meetings
— To provide our services to you as a client
— To send you information about our services that you have requested
— To improve our website and understand how visitors use it
— To comply with our legal and regulatory obligations
— To protect our legitimate business interests

We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We do not use your personal data for automated decision-making or profiling.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis for processing
sections.content:
We process your personal data on the following legal bases under Vietnam’s Law on Cybersecurity 2018, Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection, and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR):

— **Consent:** Where you have given us explicit consent to process your data (e.g., by submitting an enquiry form)
— **Contractual necessity:** Where processing is necessary to perform a contract with you or to take steps at your request before entering into a contract
— **Legitimate interests:** Where processing is necessary for our legitimate business interests, provided those interests are not overridden by your rights
— **Legal obligation:** Where we are required to process your data to comply with applicable law

sections.title: 5. How we share your personal data
sections.content:
We may share your personal data with the following categories of third parties:

— **Service providers:** Third-party vendors who help us operate our website and deliver our services (e.g., cloud hosting providers, email platforms, scheduling tools). These providers are contractually bound to process your data only on our instructions.
— **Professional advisers:** Lawyers, accountants, and auditors where necessary for our legal and compliance obligations.
— **Regulatory authorities:** Government bodies, law enforcement agencies, or courts where we are required to disclose information by law.

We do not share your personal data with any other third parties without your explicit consent.

sections.title: 6. Data retention
sections.content:
We retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected, including to satisfy any legal, accounting, or reporting requirements.

— Enquiry and contact data: up to 2 years from the date of last contact
— Client engagement data: up to 5 years from the end of the engagement, in accordance with Vietnamese business record-keeping requirements
— Website analytics data: up to 13 months on a rolling basis

When personal data is no longer required, we securely delete or anonymise it.

sections.title: 7. Your rights
sections.content:
Under Vietnam’s Decree 13/2023/ND-CP and, where applicable, the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

— **Access:** You may request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
— **Correction:** You may request that we correct any inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
— **Withdrawal of consent:** Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw your consent at any time.
— **Erasure:** In certain circumstances, you may request that we delete your personal data.
— **Data portability:** Where applicable, you may request that we provide your data in a structured, machine-readable format.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at the address below. We will respond within 30 days.

sections.title: 8. Cookies and tracking
sections.content:
Our website uses cookies and similar tracking technologies to improve your experience and collect anonymised analytics data. Cookies are small text files stored on your device.

We use the following types of cookies:
— **Essential cookies:** Required for the website to function correctly. These cannot be disabled.
— **Analytics cookies:** Used to understand how visitors interact with our website. All data collected is anonymised.

You can control cookies through your browser settings. Disabling cookies may affect the functionality of our website.

sections.title: 9. Data security
sections.content:
We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. These measures include encrypted data transmission (HTTPS), access controls, and regular security reviews.

However, no method of transmission over the internet or electronic storage is completely secure. While we strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect your personal data, we cannot guarantee absolute security.

sections.title: 10. International transfers
sections.content: Our primary operations are based in Vietnam. If we transfer your personal data outside Vietnam, we will ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place in accordance with Vietnam’s data protection requirements, including ensuring that the recipient country provides a comparable level of data protection.

sections.title: 11. Changes to this policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or applicable law. We will post the updated policy on this page with a revised effective date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

sections.title: 12. Contact us
sections.content:
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, wish to exercise your rights, or wish to make a complaint, please contact us:

**Fetch Technology Vietnam**
Email: privacy@fetch.tech
Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam

If you are not satisfied with our response, you may contact the Ministry of Public Security of Vietnam (A05 Department) regarding personal data protection matters.
